preamble: The monophthongization of the centering or rising diphthong [uo] to the close-mid back vowel [o] is a common simplification in many language families, reducing a complex nucleus to a single vowel.

examples.note: Dialectal simplification of centering diphthongs where the off-glide or on-glide is assimilated into the main vowel.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
